title: WraithConfig
description:
Top-level `wraith.toml` configuration for a single twin.

Every twin directory contains a `wraith.toml` that controls proxy
behaviour, serving parameters, conformance thresholds, model generation
settings, and recording retention policy.

A twin is an overlay iff [base] is present. Root twins omit [base].

base.description: Base twin identity (present for overlay twins, absent for root twins).

capabilities.description: Overlay capability toggles (defaults allow safe additions, prevent overrides).

overlay.description: Overlay metadata (optional, applies only when [base] is present).

passthrough.description: Passthrough mode (forward unmatched requests to upstream).

service.description: Upstream service identity and connection details.

array_length.description:
Length policy when rendering variable-length arrays. Default
`median` preserves pre-v0.7.2 behaviour; set `p90`/`max` for
bimodal/search APIs whose median array length collapses to ~0.

drop_empty_array_responses.description:
When true, responses whose every array is empty are excluded from
anti-unification for a route *as long as at least one non-empty
response exists for that route* (never prunes to zero). Makes the
median itself meaningful on bimodal corpora and benefits every
array in the response at once. Default `false` (back-compatible).

max_array_representatives.description:
Max distinct array-element representatives retained per variable-length
array. Integer N (deterministic sample of up to N distinct elements)
or `"all"`. Default `8` — byte-identical to pre-fix behaviour, so the
existing twins are unaffected unless this key is set. (bn-2gbe)

ArrayLength.description:
Array-length policy for rendering variable-length arrays from
anti-unified templates.

The renderer must pick a single length for a variable-length array.
`Median` (the historical behaviour) is correct for unimodal data but
collapses to ~0 on bimodal corpora — e.g. a debounced keystroke-search
endpoint whose recordings are dominated by empty no-match responses
with a few fat catalog loads. A high percentile or `Max` picks a
representative non-empty length so the twin serves usable data.

BaseConfig.description:
Base twin identity for overlay twins (`[base]`).

An overlay twin references a provider twin via `[base]` containing:
- `artifact`: artifact ID of the provider twin
- `digest`: content digest of the provider twin's model
- `model_schema_version`: version of the model schema used by the provider
- `scrub_policy_hash`: hash of the provider's scrub policy

`artifact` accepts two equivalent forms (bn-2j4i):

```toml
# Embedded form — matches `wraith init --base <id>@sha256:<hex>`,
# AGENTS.md, the overlay plan, and the pack/init `--help`
# examples.
[base]
artifact = "billing-api@sha256:abc..."

# Separate form — matches what `wraith init` writes to disk and
# the JSON schema documents.
[base]
artifact = "billing-api"
digest   = "sha256:abc..."
```

When both forms appear, the explicit `digest` field wins (so a
hand-edited override behaves the way an operator expects).
`model_schema_version` defaults to the current
[`SCHEMA_VERSION`] and `scrub_policy_hash` defaults to
`"UNVERIFIED"` (matching `wraith init`'s `--scrub-policy-hash`-
absent fallback), so the documented minimal `[base]` examples
(`artifact = "id@sha256:hex"` only) load without a TOML parse
error.

artifact.description:
Artifact ID of the provider twin. The bare ID (no `@sha256:`
suffix) is canonicalised here at deserialize time even when
the source TOML used the embedded form.

digest.description: Content digest (hash) of the provider twin's model.

model_schema_version.description: Schema version of the provider twin's model.

scrub_policy_hash.description: Hash of the provider twin's scrub policy.

ClockConfig.description:
Clock configuration (`[serve.clock]`).

```toml
[serve.clock]
mode = "real"            # real | deterministic | fixed
base_epoch = 1640995200  # Unix seconds; only used by deterministic / fixed
```

base_epoch.description:
Base epoch in Unix seconds. Used as the starting value for
`deterministic` mode and the frozen value for `fixed` mode.
Ignored when `mode = real`. Default = `1640995200`
(`2022-01-01T00:00:00Z`).

ClockModeConfig.description:
Clock mode for resolving timestamp holes in synthesised responses.

`real` (default) reads `SystemTime::now()` per request, so sequential
`Create` ops produce strictly-increasing `created` timestamps —
matches the behaviour of every real API. `deterministic` uses a
seed-derived monotonic counter starting from `base_epoch`, so the
same seed produces byte-identical timestamps across runs (golden
replay). `fixed` freezes the clock at `base_epoch` — preserves the
pre-bn-2y5b legacy behaviour for callers that depend on a stable
timestamp value.

oneOf.description: Seed-derived monotonic counter, advances per read.

DependentCaseAllowRule.description:
One allowlisted dependent-case target pair.

Example:

```toml
[[generate.dependent_cases.allow]]
route = "GET /v1/events"
discriminator = "$.data[*].type"
target = "$.data[*].data"
```

discriminator.description: Sibling string discriminator path in `$`-rooted response JSON form.

route.description: Route pattern to match, e.g. `"GET /v1/events"`. Absent = any route.

target.description: Target object path selected by the discriminator.

DependentCaseInferenceConfig.description:
Experimental dependent-case synthesis (`[generate.dependent_cases]`).

Disabled by default. When enabled, synth only emits dependent-case metadata
for explicit allowlist entries. This keeps broad MDL candidates from
changing production artifacts unless an operator names the route + target
pair to try.

allow.description: Explicit allowed route + discriminator + target pairs.

enabled.description: Master gate for dependent-case inference. Default `false`.

max_cases.description: Maximum distinct discriminator values accepted for one target pair.

min_gain_bits.description: Minimum model-compression gain in bits required to emit metadata.

min_observations.description: Minimum valid target observations before an allowlisted pair can emit.

fields.description:
User-supplied field classifications keyed by JSON path (e.g. `$.id`).

These are merged with auto-detected classifications from the synth
model, with user-supplied values taking precedence.

ignore_headers.description:
Extra response headers to ignore during conformance checks.

These are merged with the built-in ignore list (date, server,
x-request-id, etc.). Case-insensitive.

split_variants.description:
Enable discriminator-based variant splitting during synthesis.

When `true`, `wraith synth` splits heterogeneous endpoints by
discriminator field (type/object/kind) into per-type variants with
inferred path-segment guards.  Enable per-twin when the API serves
different resource types through the same route pattern.

suppress.description:
Suppressed divergences — known mismatches to exclude from reports
and scoring.

```toml
[[diff.suppress]]
route = "POST /repos/*/statuses/*"
path = "body.created_at"
reason = "twin uses placeholder timestamps"

[[diff.suppress]]
category = "header_mismatch"
reason = "vendor headers not reproduced by twin"

[[diff.suppress]]
route = "POST /v1/chat/completions"
path = "status"
category = "status_mismatch"
expected = "2xx"
actual = "4xx"
reason = "OpenAI-compatible invalid-model errors vary by vendor"
```

tagged_union_policy.description:
Per-route / per-scope fallback policy for unknown tagged-union tags.

Each rule overrides the synthesized (default `reject_unknown`)
[`FallbackPolicy`](crate::cli::synth::FallbackPolicy) for tagged-union
scopes that match its `route`/`scope` globs. The default stays
fail-closed; rules opt specific scopes into looser handling for APIs
(such as Stripe) that continually add discriminator values.

```toml
[[diff.tagged_union_policy]]
route = "GET /v1/events"
scope = "body.data[*].data.object"
policy = "fallback_common_schema"
reason = "Stripe adds event types continuously"
```

DivergenceSuppression.description:
A suppression rule for known divergences.

All non-`None` fields must match for a divergence to be suppressed.
Fields left unset (or absent in TOML) act as wildcards.

```toml
[[diff.suppress]]
route = "POST /repos/*/statuses/*"
path = "body.created_at"
reason = "twin uses placeholder timestamps"

[[diff.suppress]]
route = "POST /v1/chat/completions"
path = "status"
category = "status_mismatch"
expected = "2xx"
actual = "4xx"
reason = "OpenAI-compatible invalid-model errors vary by vendor"
```

actual.description:
Actual value pattern to match. Supports `*` globs and HTTP status
class patterns such as `"2xx"` and `"4xx"`.

expected.description:
Expected value pattern to match. Supports `*` globs and HTTP status
class patterns such as `"2xx"` and `"4xx"`.

reason.description: Human-readable reason for suppression (not used in matching).

route.description:
Route pattern to match. Supports `*` as a wildcard segment.
Example: `"POST /repos/*/statuses/*"` or `"GET /v1/users/:param"`.

FieldClassConfig.description:
User-supplied field classification for conformance diffing.

Controls how a specific JSON path is compared during conformance checks.

```toml
[diff.fields."$.id"]
classify = "generated"

[diff.fields."$.status"]
classify = "enum"
values = ["active", "inactive", "deleted"]
```

llm.description: LLM provider configuration for agent-driven generation.

GuardInferenceConfig.description: Guard inference for conditional response logic (`[generate.guard_inference]`).

IdempotencyConfig.description:
Idempotency-Key replay cache configuration (`[serve.idempotency]`).

When `enabled` is true, POST requests carrying the configured header
(default: `Idempotency-Key`) get their successful (2xx) responses cached
per `(route_pattern, header_value)` tuple within the request's session
namespace. Repeat POSTs with the same key return the cached body / status
/ headers verbatim (Date is refreshed on the way out by the renderer).

Default is `enabled = false` so non-Stripe twins don't inadvertently get
this behaviour surprise — opt-in per twin in `wraith.toml`.

```toml
[serve.idempotency]
enabled = true
header = "Idempotency-Key"
ttl_seconds = 86400
```

See bn-300b.

enabled.description: Whether the idempotency cache is enabled. Default `false`.

header.description:
HTTP header carrying the idempotency key. Stripe convention is
`Idempotency-Key`; some APIs use other names.

ttl_seconds.description:
Cache entry TTL in seconds. Entries past this age are evicted lazily
on lookup (treated as misses). Default 86400 (24h).

LimitsConfig.description: Resource limits for the twin server (`[serve.limits]`).

LlmConfig.description: LLM provider configuration from `[generate.llm]` in wraith.toml.

api_key.description: API key for openai provider. Overridden by `WRAITH_LLM_API_KEY` env var.

LuaConfig.description:
Lua handler execution policy (`[serve.lua]`).

Controls how the runtime reacts when a Lua handler raises an uncaught
error mid-execution (string arithmetic, nil dereference, type error,
`error(...)` call, runtime budget exceeded, etc.). See bn-1viq.

```toml
[serve.lua]
on_error = "fail"     # 500 + JSON error envelope (DEFAULT)
# on_error = "fallback"  # legacy: drop the error, fall through to template
```

on_error.description: Behaviour on uncaught handler error. Default = `fail`.

LuaOnError.description:
What to do when a Lua handler raises an uncaught error.

`Fail` (the default since bn-1viq) returns HTTP 500 with a structured
JSON envelope describing the failure — the failure is visible to
clients and to test assertions on status code. `Fallback` falls through
to the synth template engine, producing a synthetic response (often a
201 Created with a random id). The template fallback hid real handler
bugs from conformance tests, which is why it is now opt-in.

oneOf.description: Return HTTP 500 with a JSON error envelope. **Default.**

Log the error and fall through to template rendering (legacy
pre-bn-1viq behaviour). Opt-in for compatibility.

MaxArrayReps.description:
How many distinct array-element *representatives* anti-unification
retains for a variable-length array.

Length fidelity (`array_length`) and element *variety* are independent
axes. `array_length = p90` recovers a ~500-long `content[]`, but if only
8 distinct elements are retained the renderer tiles those 8 to length —
list-heavy UIs then show 8 rows repeated ~62×. This knob raises (or
removes) that cap. `Count(8)` is the historical default and is
byte-identical to pre-fix behaviour; raise it (or set `"all"`) for
catalog/search APIs whose recordings carry many distinct rows.

Accepts an integer (`max_array_representatives = 500`) or the string
`"all"` (`max_array_representatives = "all"`). The integer form is a
deterministic, size-bounded sample of up to N distinct elements in
first-seen order — i.e. the "sample up to K" and "configurable cap with
all" behaviours are one mechanism, not two.

oneOf.description:
Retain a deterministic sample of up to N distinct elements
(first-seen order). `Count(8)` is the back-compatible default.

OverlayCapabilities.description:
Capability toggles for overlay twins (`[capabilities]`).

Controls what modifications an overlay is permitted to make to its
provider twin's model. All fields default to reasonable values to prevent
accidental data loss.

add_fault_profiles.description: Allow adding new fault profiles. Default `true`.

add_fixture_sets.description: Allow adding new fixture sets. Default `true`.

add_lua_handlers.description: Allow adding Lua handlers. Default `false` (disabled by default for safety).

add_schema_extensions.description: Allow adding schema extensions (new fields, new types). Default `true`.

add_variants.description: Allow adding new variants to existing routes. Default `true`.

override_lua_handlers.description: Allow overriding existing Lua handlers. Default `false`.

OverlayConfig.description:
Overlay metadata (`[overlay]`).

Optional metadata describing the overlay twin, including ownership
and dependencies on specific provider capabilities.

description.description: Description of the overlay's purpose and scope.

owner.description: Human-readable owner of the overlay (e.g. team name).

PassthroughConfig.description: Passthrough mode: forward unmatched requests to upstream (`[passthrough]`).

oneOf.description: Forward requests to the upstream host (same host/port used as target).
Start a forward proxy with proxy-style request handling.
Use forward proxy mode with explicit TLS interception.

RateLimitEntry.description: A single rate-limit rule for a route pattern.

limit.description: Maximum requests allowed in the window.

pack_mode.description:
Evidence level when packaging this twin as a `.wraith` archive.

`none` (default): model only — suitable for internal CI.
`manifests`: session metadata included; no WREC binary data.
`scrubbed-full`: all recordings re-scrubbed and sealed; required for
`wraith check --semantic` and cross-team promotion.

required_for_promotion.description:
Whether recordings are required before this twin can be promoted to a
shared registry. When `true` (the default), `wraith promote` requires
`pack_mode = "scrubbed-full"` or an explicit external evidence location.

RecordingsPackMode.description:
Recording evidence level when packaging a twin as a `.wraith` archive.

Controls which recording files `wraith pack` includes. Defaults to `none`.

```toml
[recordings]
pack_mode = "none"           # no recordings (CI default)
# pack_mode = "manifests"    # session metadata only, no WREC binary data
# pack_mode = "scrubbed-full" # all recordings, scrubbed
```

oneOf.description: No recording files in the archive. Default for internal CI consumption.
Session metadata files only (e.g. `manifest.json`). WREC binary data excluded.
All recording files, re-scrubbed per the twin's `scrub.toml` before sealing.

fields.description:
Request-body JSON paths (`$.a.b.c`) whose values key the response.
Multiple fields form a composite key (`And` of `FieldEquals`), e.g.
`["$.bulksearchv1AssetsInput.useCase",
  "$.bulksearchv1AssetsInput.filter.parentId"]`.

route.description:
`"METHOD /path/pattern"` (the synth path pattern, e.g.
`"POST /v1/assets/actions/search"`; `:param` segments as synthesised).
Explicit per-route rules. Honoured under both `manual` and `auto`.

RequestKeyingMode.description:
Request-keyed response bucketing mode (`[generate.request_keying] mode`).

When a route's response depends on a *request* field (a parent id, a
`useCase` scope, a search filter) synth otherwise collapses every value
to one global representative — expanding any tree node returns the same
payload. This selects how synth recovers request→response correlation.

oneOf.description:
No request keying — pre-bn-3af5 behaviour. Default (inert: the 23
existing twins are unaffected unless this is changed).

Explicit rules are honoured; routes without a rule additionally get
a conservative auto-detected request key when one strongly predicts
the response.

RunnerConfig.description: Configuration for a single LLM runner process.

RunnerRoutingConfig.description: LLM runner routing for agent-assisted synthesis (`[generate.routing]`).

SampleStrategy.description: Sample strategy used during refresh planning.

clock.description:
Clock-resolution policy for `created` / `created_at` / `updated_at`
hole rendering (`[serve.clock]`). See [`ClockConfig`] and bn-2y5b.

idempotency.description:
Idempotency-Key replay cache (`[serve.idempotency]`). Disabled by
default — non-Stripe twins shouldn't get this behaviour surprise.
See [`IdempotencyConfig`] and bn-300b.

limits.description: Resource limits for the twin server.

lua.description:
Lua handler execution policy (`[serve.lua]`). Controls what happens
when a Lua handler raises an uncaught error during execution. The
default (`on_error = "fail"`) is to return a 500 with a structured
JSON error envelope — silent template fallback turns broken handlers
into looks-like-success 201s and hid the bug fixed in bn-1viq. Set
`on_error = "fallback"` to opt back into the legacy behaviour.
See [`LuaConfig`] and bn-1viq.

port.description: Port the twin server listens on (default: 8081).

rewrite_self_urls.description:
Rewrite absolute upstream URLs in response bodies and the
`Location:` header so they point back at the twin (default
`true`).

When `true`, any URL-shaped string in the response whose host
matches `service.base_url` gets its scheme + host swapped to
match the incoming request's `Host` header. This stops clients
from leaking off the twin and hitting the recorded upstream
when they follow next-page links, related-resource URLs, etc.
See bn-2qi3.

strip_headers.description:
Strip non-allowlisted response headers on the way out (default `true`).

When `true` (the default — see bn-2qi3), the twin only sends back
a small set of safe headers (`content-type`, `www-authenticate`,
`proxy-authenticate`, `location`, `content-encoding`,
`content-language`, and standard CORS headers). Vendor headers
the twin replays from the recorded upstream — Cloudflare's
`cf-*`, `x-cache`, `x-served-by`, Express's `x-powered-by`,
`nel`/`report-to`, etc. — get dropped because they confuse
client middleware (CORS, NEL, retry).

Set to `false` to replay every header from the recording
verbatim — useful when debugging upstream behavior, but not
recommended for normal client testing.

ServeFidelityMode.description: Response fidelity mode used by runtime serving.

oneOf.description: Legacy permissive mode; behavior is not yet implemented.

ServeLatencyMode.description: Simulated latency mode used for runtime latency simulation.

oneOf.description: Replay recorded latencies for matched routes.

base_url.description: Base URL of the upstream API (e.g. `https://api.stripe.com`).

name.description: Human-readable twin name (used as directory name under `twins/`).

spec.description: Optional path to an `OpenAPI` spec file.

user_agent.description: User-Agent header string for HTTP requests (default: `wraith/0.1.7`).

SessionMode.description: Session isolation mode used for per-request state segmentation.

oneOf.description: One session per API credential in a shared HTTP header.

SymbolizationConfig.description: Symbolisation heuristics for identifying dynamic values (`[generate.symbolization]`).

TaggedUnionPolicyRule.description:
Operator override of the unknown-tag fallback policy for tagged unions.

Targets one or more tagged-union scopes by `route` and/or `scope` glob
and replaces the synthesized (default `reject_unknown`)
[`FallbackPolicy`](crate::cli::synth::FallbackPolicy). Unset fields act as
wildcards; the default behavior (no rule) stays fail-closed.

```toml
[[diff.tagged_union_policy]]
route = "GET /v1/events"
scope = "body.data[*].data.object"
policy = "fallback_common_schema"
reason = "Stripe adds event types continuously"
```

policy.description:
Policy to apply: `reject_unknown`, `fallback_common_schema`, or
`replay_representative`. Unrecognized values are ignored with a warning.

reason.description: Human-readable reason for the override (not used in matching).

route.description:
Route pattern to match. Supports `*` as a wildcard segment.
Example: `"GET /v1/events"`. Absent = any route.

scope.description:
Union scope path in differ form (e.g. `"body.data[*].data.object"`).
Supports `*` globs. Absent = any scope.

TypeInferenceConfig.description: Type inference for enum and pattern detection (`[generate.type_inference]`).
